source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: In Wrangell Narrows, Alaska
description: Historic gelatin silver postcard of a small conifer-covered islet in Wrangell Narrows, Alaska — the calm protected waterway that forms part of the Inside Passage connecting Ketchikan, Wrangell, and Petersburg. The scene is characteristic of the Southeast Alaska temperate rainforest coast that surrounds Misty Fjords National Monument. Produced by the Thwaites Studio, card number 2007, between 1900 and 1940.
alt: Historic sepia postcard of a small tree-covered island in Wrangell Narrows, Alaska with calm protected water and forested shorelines
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from tile.loc.gov and visually confirmed
